preamble: The phonetic transformation from the voiceless alveolar fricative /s/ to the voiced postalveolar affricate /d͡ʒ/ is an attested historical sound change, notably observed in the development of the Hruso language from Proto-Tibeto-Burman. This shift involves a combination of phonetic processes, including palatalization, voicing, and affrication.
phoneticEffects: This shift entails palatalization, where the point of articulation moves from the alveolar ridge to the postalveolar region
sources: Post, M. A. (2011). First notes on the phonology and classification of the Bangru language of India. Himalayan Linguistics, 10(1), 1-28.
